title: cookie.serialize with httpOnly: false — the auth cookie is readable from JavaScript (XSS session theft)

params.passDetail: cookie.serialize sets httpOnly: true — the cookie is not exposed to document.cookie, so client-side scripts cannot read the token.
params.failDetail: cookie.serialize is called with httpOnly: false, so the Set-Cookie it builds is readable by JavaScript via document.cookie. Any XSS on the origin can exfiltrate the session/auth token and hijack the account. Set httpOnly: true for authentication cookies.
params.absentCallDetail: No cookie.serialize call in this scope; this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: cookie.serialize does not set httpOnly; verify auth cookies are serialized with httpOnly: true.

provenance.note: The low-level `cookie` package underlies most Node cookie handling. httpOnly: false is set so front-end JS can read the token, trading the primary XSS-session-theft mitigation for convenience.
